home.hero_subtitle: The charm of a farmhouse, thoughtfully restored with comfort
home.history_p1: Built in 1803 by Michel Cachat, Le Solerey was originally a Savoyard farmhouse in the heart of Le Lavancher. The central ground-floor room housed livestock, while the upper floor was used for hay storage.
home.history_p2: Now fully restored, this authentic farmhouse has preserved its original materials — stone, aged wood, exposed beams — while offering the modern comforts of a luxury chalet.
home.seo_title: A 400 m² chalet at the foot of Mont-Blanc
home.seo_p1: Ideally located in Le Lavancher, in the Chamonix-Mont-Blanc valley, Le Solerey offers an exceptional setting for family or group stays. At 1200 m altitude, sunny and overlooking the valley, Le Lavancher offers a stunning view of the needles and the Mont-Blanc massif.
home.seo_p2: With 8 bedrooms, 4 bathrooms, 3 living rooms with distinct atmospheres, a private sauna and a billiard room, Le Solerey is one of the rare chalets in Chamonix capable of accommodating large groups without compromising individual comfort.
home.seo_p3: From the chalet, you can quickly reach all the valley's ski resorts: Les Houches, Le Brévent, La Flégère, Les Grands Montets in Argentière, and Le Tour. The Chamonix bus, free with your guest card, serves the entire area.
home.seo_p4: In winter, Le Solerey is the ideal base for a skiing holiday in Chamonix. In summer, the valley offers exceptional terrain for hiking, mountaineering, mountain biking, paragliding or simply admiring the Mont-Blanc massif.
home.cta_subtitle: For any reservation, availability or information request

history.s1_p1: Built in 1803 by Michel Cachat, Le Solerey was originally a farmhouse. The central ground-floor room (the stable) housed livestock. Only one third was habitable, around the kitchen and its large fireplace.
history.s1_p2: It also contains the living room with its stove, used as both a lounge and bedroom. The upper floor was reserved for hay storage. At that time, the main activities of the five farms in Le Lavancher were livestock farming and growing potatoes as a staple food.
history.s1_p3: Gathered around the bread oven, they gradually witnessed the growing enthusiasm for the mountains. Le Lavancher was the essential path to reach the Vallée Blanche via Le Chapeau and the Mauvais Pas. This is how Le Solerey came to house the shoemaker of the alpinists.
history.s2_p1: At that time, tourism was in full swing, although the traditional activities of Le Lavancher, focused on field farming, remained unchanged. Hotels such as the Hôtel Beau-Séjour appeared to welcome skiers returning from the Vallée Blanche.
history.s2_p2: In 1905, Chamonix Mont Blanc experienced a cold record with temperatures dropping to -31 degrees. In 1908, the first section of the Montenvers railway was inaugurated, providing access to the Mer de Glace. The Winter Olympics marked the year 1924.
history.s3_p: At 1200 m altitude, sunny and overlooking the valley, Le Lavancher offers a breathtaking view of the needles: an ideal spot to escape the hustle of Chamonix. Central point of the three ski areas, crossed by the cross-country ski trail, Le Lavancher is also the starting point for many hikes.

activities.hero_subtitle: Hiking, climbing, restaurants, local events... Discover our recommendations to make the most of the Chamonix-Mont-Blanc valley.
activities.recommended_intro: The usual advice is to take the Aiguille du Midi or visit the Montenvers to explore the Chamonix valley. Here we share more personal experiences that left a mark on us — less touristy but equally breathtaking.
activities.act_chapeau_desc: The hike starts from the top of Le Lavancher, just above the Chalets Philippe. At the end of the walk, you'll find a refreshment stand with a magnificent view of the needles.
activities.act_philippe_desc: A picturesque spot at the top of Le Lavancher, perfect for a drink or dinner. The view is breathtaking and the atmosphere is authentic.
activities.act_pain_desc: This event brings together all of Chamonix around activities organised in each village of the valley. In Le Lavancher, the bread oven is fired for three days to bake bread for the big day.
activities.act_praz_desc: This easy hike starts below Le Lavancher, along the Arve river. It's a route leading to Chamonix, offering a leisure area.
activities.act_gorges_desc: The hike follows the Chapeau trail. A relatively easy walk leading to a waterfall with a stunning view of the valley. Along the way you can also stop at the climbing slabs (15 routes, 60 m height).
activities.act_buet_desc: The hike departs from the village of Buet, where you'll discover the charming Bérart waterfall with a refreshment stand. At the summit, a 360-degree panoramic view of the Mont Blanc massif, the Northern Alps and the Swiss Alps.
activities.act_albert_desc: The refuge is accessed via the Tour ski lifts, located on the Tour glacier plateau. It offers a 180-degree panoramic view of the Chardonnay, Argentière, Tour needles, and Mont-Blanc.
activities.act_lacblanc_desc: Ranked among the ten most beautiful places in France, this hike combines an exceptional panoramic view with a nature reserve where ibex and marmots can be spotted. Depart from Argentière or the Flégère lifts.
activities.act_emosson_desc: At an altitude of 1950 metres, the site is accessible by car. It is also the starting point of a hike to the Émossons refuge, where dinosaur footprints can be seen.
activities.act_nid_desc: To reach the refuge, you can take the Mont-Blanc tramway. The descent runs along the Bonnassay glacier, opening onto a valley where horses roam freely in a magical landscape.

footer.description: Authentic restored farmhouse in Le Lavancher, accommodating up to 14 guests, 10 min from the Chamonix-Mont-Blanc ski slopes.
footer.copyright: Chalet Le Solerey — Le Lavancher, Chamonix-Mont-Blanc
